channel.title: Data Breach & Ransomware Aggregator
channel.description: Real-time data breach notifications from state registries, federal sources, and ransomware trackers

item.title: Dialogflow CX 'Rogue Agent' Flaw Enabled AI Chatbot Data Theft
item.description:
Varonis reported the flaw to Google in late 2025 and it has been addressed, but it reminds defenders to take a fresh look at their AI Infrastructure security.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Lone Attacker Uses AI to Breach AWS Cloud Environment in 72 Hours
item.description:
The attacker exploited AI workflows, chained cloud weaknesses, and stolen credentials to extort a large Amazon customer.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Mexico's New Cyber Plan Faces Its First Real Test
item.description:
The Latin American nation's cybersecurity plan — still in the expansion phase — has to survive its own knockout round during the FIFA World Cup.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: European Organizations Have a Collaboration Security Confidence Gap
item.description:
A new survey shows security leaders have an inflated sense of safety regarding their collaboration tools and platforms.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: 'GodDamn' Ransomware Uses BYOVD to Smite US Companies
item.description:
Microsoft signed a malicious kernel driver, and now it's being used to kill security software in ransomware attacks.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: AI Gateways Offer Attackers the Keys to the Kingdom
item.description:
A cryptomining incident highlights how AI gateways can provide access to AI models, cloud infrastructure, and identity and access management (IAM) data.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Microsoft Reins in RoguePlanet Zero-Day Threat
item.description:
The researcher known as "Nightmare-Eclipse" published a proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit for the Windows Defender vulnerability in early June after dropping several other Microsoft zero-days.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Iran's Cyber Crosshairs Focus Beyond Critical Infrastructure
item.description:
Obscurity isn't a defense. If your company has any Internet-facing vulnerability, you're at risk from multiple threats.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: AI Coding: Do Security Risks Outweigh Productivity Gains?
item.description:
AI coding tools cost $19-$200/month/user, but security scanning, remediation, and false positives add hidden costs. Are the productivity gains worth it?
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Cybercriminals Flock to Healthcare Businesses as Attacks Surge
item.description:
While cyberattacks against hospitals and clinics grew modestly in the first half of 2026, attacks on service providers and other healthcare businesses more than doubled.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Jen Ellis: Connecting Cyber Community With Political Machinery
item.description:
Security Pro File: On the heels of her recent honors as a Member of the Order of the British Empire (MBE), we take a look back at the events that shaped Jen Ellis' advocacy on behalf of security researchers.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: QIZ Security Raises $17 Million for Cryptographic Governance Platform
item.description:
The Israeli company has developed a cryptographic posture and post-quantum cryptography management platform. The post QIZ Security Raises $17 Million for Cryptographic Governance Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Network of 200 GitHub Repositories Used for Malware Infection
item.description:
A Go module is used to load PowerShell code that fetches a resolver from public dead drops to execute Windows malware. The post Network of 200 GitHub Repositories Used for Malware Infection appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: GigaWiper Combines Multiple Malware for System-Level Sabotage
item.description:
The backdoor’s destructive capabilities include a standalone wiper, ransomware encryption, and a multi-pass wiping command. The post GigaWiper Combines Multiple Malware for System-Level Sabotage appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Okta Warns of Vishing Attacks Targeting Microsoft 365 Customers
item.description:
The attackers call victims to direct them to phishing websites mirroring Microsoft Entra ID login pages. The post Okta Warns of Vishing Attacks Targeting Microsoft 365 Customers appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: China, India-Linked Hackers Both Targeted Same Pakistani Police Force
item.description:
Both foes and allies have targeted the Balochistan Police force in Pakistan for at least two years, according to SentinelOne. The post China, India-Linked Hackers Both Targeted Same Pakistani Police Force appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Third US Security Expert Sentenced to Prison for Helping Ransomware Gang
item.description:
Angelo Martino, a former ransomware negotiator, was sentenced to 70 months for helping the BlackCat/Alphv group. The post Third US Security Expert Sentenced to Prison for Helping Ransomware Gang appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: In Other News: DHS Database Hacked, Adobe Boosts Patch Cadence, Canada Disrupts Ransomware Ops
item.description:
Other noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: Abnormal AI sued by Anthropic, AssuranceAmerica data breach affects 7 million people, NSA brings back TAO. The post In Other News: DHS Database Hacked, Adobe Boosts Patch Cadence, Canada Disrupts Ransomware Ops appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Dutch police trace Odido telco cyberattack to suspected local accomplice
item.description:
Dutch police said Thursday they had uncovered evidence suggesting that Dutch criminals were involved in the cyberattack on telecom provider Odido that exposed the personal data of more than 6 million customers earlier this year.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
item.description:
Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and a vast trove of other agency secrets on a public GitHub account. The inquiry comes as CISA is still struggling to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
item.description:
Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation campaigns inside the European Union. The two men were the focus of a 2025 KrebsOnSecurity story about how their hosting companies had assumed control over the technical infrastructure of Stark Industries Solutions, an Internet service provider sanctioned last year by the EU as a frequ
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
item.description:
The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting account passwords.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Who Runs the Ransomware Group ‘The Gentlemen?’
item.description:
A cybercrime group known as The Gentlemen has emerged as the second most active ransomware gang by victim count, rapidly attracting a talented pool of hackers through an aggressive recruitment strategy that promises affiliates 90 percent of any ransom paid by victims. This post examines clues pointing to a real life identity for the administrator of The Gentlemen ransomware group.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
item.description:
Two men pleaded guilty in the United Kingdom this week to criminal charges stemming from an August 2024 cyberattack that crippled Transport for London, the entity responsible for the public transport network in the Greater London area. The duo were key members of a prolific cybercrime group known as Scattered Spider, and their guilty pleas came on the first day of what was expected to be a six-week trial.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Felons, Fraudsters Flog Offensive Cybersecurity Startup
item.description:
A cybersecurity startup dangling millions of dollars to acquire zero-day security vulnerabilities in popular software is run by a pair of far-right conspiracy theorists and convicted felons whose most recent ventures included fake intelligence companies and a now-defunct AI-based lobbying platform they operated under assumed names.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Calibrated Healthcare Settles Class Action Data Breach Lawsuit
item.description:
Calibrated Healthcare Systems, LLC, and Calibrated Healthcare, LLC, have agreed to settle a class action lawsuit stemming from a February […] The post Calibrated Healthcare Settles Class Action Data Breach Lawsuit appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Free Webinar: AI + HIPAA: Innovating in Healthcare Without Leaving Compliance Behind
item.description:
Artificial intelligence has tremendous potential in healthcare, and healthcare organizations have embraced AI tools in all areas of their operation; […] The post Free Webinar: AI + HIPAA: Innovating in Healthcare Without Leaving Compliance Behind appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Data Security Incidents Announced by Park Dental Research Corp; Wabi Sabi Behavioral Health Center
item.description:
Employee data has been compromised in data security incidents at Park Dental Research Corporation in Oklahoma and Wabi Sabi Behavioral […] The post Data Security Incidents Announced by Park Dental Research Corp; Wabi Sabi Behavioral Health Center appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: HIPAA Security Rule Update Postponed: More Time Given to Implement Major HIPAA Security Rule Changes
item.description:
There has been some good news for the HIPAA-regulated entities that feel unprepared for the proposed changes to the HIPAA […] The post HIPAA Security Rule Update Postponed: More Time Given to Implement Major HIPAA Security Rule Changes appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: ERMI; McLeod Physician Associates; Centers for Dialysis Care Announce Data Breaches
item.description:
Data breaches have been announced by the Georgia-based medical equipment company ERMI, McLeod Physician Associates in South Carolina, and the […] The post ERMI; McLeod Physician Associates; Centers for Dialysis Care Announce Data Breaches appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Drug and Alcohol Treatment Services Settles Data Breach Litigation
item.description:
Drug and Alcohol Treatment Services, Inc., a Scranton, Pennsylvania-based provider of drug and alcohol addiction services, has agreed to settle […] The post Drug and Alcohol Treatment Services Settles Data Breach Litigation appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Accenture Confirms Intrusion After Hacker Claims 35GB Data Breach
item.description:
Accenture, one of the world’s largest consulting firms, has confirmed it has experienced a security breach, shortly after a hacker […] The post Accenture Confirms Intrusion After Hacker Claims 35GB Data Breach appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Aitkin County Health and Human Services Data Breach Affects 81,000 Individuals
item.description:
Aitkin County Health and Human Services in Minnesota has identified a breach of its email environment. Data breaches have also […] The post Aitkin County Health and Human Services Data Breach Affects 81,000 Individuals appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: New Helix vishing group emerges in SharePoint data theft attacks
item.description:
A new data-extortion group called Helix is using identity-focused tactics such as voice phishing (vishing), device code phishing, and multi-factor authentication (MFA) abuse to steal data from SharePoint environments. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Former ransomware negotiator gets 4 years for BlackCat attacks
item.description:
A former employee of cybersecurity incident response company DigitalMint was sentenced to 70 months in prison for targeting U.S. companies in BlackCat (ALPHV) ransomware attacks. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Hackers exploit critical auth bypass in Gitea Docker image
item.description:
Hackers are actively exploiting a critical vulnerability in the official Docker image for the Gitea self-hosted Git service that allows attackers to impersonate any user, including administrators. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Police suspects Dutch hackers were involved in Odido breach
item.description:
The Dutch National Police (Politie) says it has found "strong indications" that Dutch hackers have been involved in a February breach at the telecommunications provider Odido. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Ryuk ransomware member pleads guilty in the US, faces 15 years in prison
item.description:
A 34-year-old Armenian man has pleaded guilty to hacking U.S. companies and deploying the infamous Ryuk ransomware to encrypt their systems. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: New U-Boot flaws could enable stealthy firmware attacks
item.description:
Six vulnerabilities in the widely used U-Boot bootloader have been discovered that could allow attackers to execute malicious code during device boot, potentially enabling stealthy firmware attacks that compromise security protections and install persistent malware. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Alberta, Centurion Project sued over alleged data breach that affected millions of voters
item.description:
Carrie Tait reports: A retired lawyer is suing Alberta, its Chief Electoral Officer and two organizations that support secession for their respective roles in an alleged data breach affecting 2.9 million residents in the province. Clint Docken, a former class-action lawyer, last week filed a sweeping lawsuit stemming from allegations the Centurion Project unlawfully obtained... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: The “Anonymous” Tip System That Wasn’t: Three Months Later, Why Hasn’t Navigate360 Notified Anyone?
item.description:
Trigger Warning: This post includes content from tips submitted to anonymous tiplines by or about students. While identity information is redacted, tips may include obscenities and explicit references to sexual abuse, rape, assault, self-harm, violence, suicidal ideation, pornography, and pedophilia. Overview On March 18, 2026, DDoSecrets and Straight Arrow News reported on a dataset provided... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Jacksonville, Texas, keeps some city systems offline after cyber incident
item.description:
DysruptionHub reports: Jacksonville, Texas, took some city systems offline after detecting suspicious network activity Friday, leaving some online services unavailable Monday as officials investigated a cybersecurity incident. The city said it detected the activity July 3 and later determined it was related to a cybersecurity incident. Officials took affected systems offline, disabled some systems as... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Bojangles sued again by workers over Russian hacker data breach. NC judge weighs in
item.description:
Chase Jordan reports an update in the litigation stemming from a 2024 breach by Hunters International. This case has raised a number of issues about standing and negligence and has been up and down in the courts, with plaintiffs seeming to fare better in state court: A class-action lawsuit over a 2024 data breach of Bojangles employee... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Washington Dept. of Social and Health Services announces massive data breach
item.description:
KIRO7 reports: The Washington Department of Social and Health Services (DSHS) is issuing a notice of a massive data breach that happened in March, potentially compromising the personal data of around 8,600 people. An internal investigation revealed that a former DSHS employee accessed the data without authorization. Some of the data the employee improperly potentially... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Why Bangladesh’s new data protection law may fail to protect your data
item.description:
Meem Arafat Manab reports: On August 19, 2025, hackers broke into Shwapno’s customer database. They took 410 gigabytes of data: the names, phone numbers, and purchase histories of forty lakh registered customers. They demanded $1.5 million. Shwapno refused, secured its systems, and said nothing to its customers for seven months. When the hackers published the... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Patients Sue Healthcare Corporations Over Data Breaches, Sharing of Personal Information
item.description:
Mikeie Honda Reiland reports: A suite of recent class action lawsuits in state and federal courts seeks to hold large healthcare corporations accountable for exposing or leaking patients’ personally identifiable information (PII) and protected health information (PHI). On June 11 in Davidson County Circuit Court, three Jane Does filed suit against CareNow, which operates more... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Uniondale Union Free School District – Audit Follow-Up by New York State Comptroller (2023M-61-F)
item.description:
In October 2023, NYS Comptroller Thomas DiNapoli released an IT audit of the Uniondale Union Free School District on Long Island. The purpose of the audit was to examine management of non-student user network controls. The audit report found, in part: District officials did not adequately manage nonstudent network user accounts and permissions. As a... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Nayax investigating breach; The Syndicate claims it acquired 1 billion card records and other important data
item.description:
Nayax is a global fintech company headquartered in Israel that provides cashless payment and management solutions for unattended retail and self-service machines. The firm is publicly traded on both the Tel Aviv and Nasdaq stock exchanges. This month, Nayax submitted a Form 6-K to the Securities and Exchange Commission. The form contained an “Explanatory Statement:”... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Two arrests this week in unrelated crimes involved Japanese teenagers using ChatGPT to assist in their crimes
item.description:
The Japan Times reports: An 18-year-old man has been arrested for his suspected involvement in a cyberattack on the operator of the Kaikatsu Club internet cafe chain, according to investigative sources. On Wednesday, the Metropolitan Police Department’s cybercrime countermeasure division arrested the company employee from Tokyo’s Katsushika Ward, who was in the second year of... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Sintax Report Victim sintax.be Threat Actor Qilin Date Discovered Jul 10, 2026 Description Sintax is a boutique law firm based in Belgium, provides tax law services, wealth â¦July 10, 2026
item.description:
Sintax Report Victim sintax.be Threat Actor Qilin Date Discovered Jul 10, 2026 Description Sintax is a boutique law firm based in Belgium, provides tax law services, wealth â¦July 10, 2026 reported a ransomware. Attributed to Qilin.
🎭 Threat Actor: Qilin | 📰 Source: BreachSense | 🏷️ Type: Ransomware

item.title: Robroy Industries Report Victim robroy.com Threat Actor BrainCipher Date Discovered Jul 10, 2026 Description Robroy Industries is a manufacturing company based in the USA â¦July 10, 2026
item.description:
Robroy Industries Report Victim robroy.com Threat Actor BrainCipher Date Discovered Jul 10, 2026 Description Robroy Industries is a manufacturing company based in the USA â¦July 10, 2026 reported a ransomware. Attributed to Qilin.
🎭 Threat Actor: Qilin | 📰 Source: BreachSense | 🏷️ Type: Ransomware

item.title: Pinturas PRISA Report Victim prisa.mx Threat Actor AiLock Date Discovered Jul 10, 2026 Description Pinturas PRISA is a coatings manufacturer based in Mexico, specializing in â¦July 10, 2026

item.description:
Blackwater Ransomware Attack on txdkj.com Exposes Critical Data reported a ransomware.
